note: Informational only. Survivors are candidate gaps to confirm against the frozen spec; the equivalent-mutant judge only pre screens them. Not a gate; the mutmut baseline (mutation/baseline.json) is the authoritative number.

mutants.desc: Void releases the full original authorized_amount instead of the remaining held balance after a partial capture, over-crediting external settlement for funds already captured.
mutants.expect: INV: hold release on void must equal authorized_amount - captured_amount (no double release of already-captured funds)

equivalent.reason: When an intent is partially captured before void, the mutant releases the full authorized_amount instead of only the uncaptured remainder, double-releasing already-captured funds and producing a different ledger balance.

mutants.desc: Refundable amount ignores prior refunds, so repeated partial refunds can cumulatively exceed captured_amount, driving the merchant account balance negative.
mutants.expect: INV-3: cumulative refunded_amount must never exceed captured_amount; merchant balance must never go negative

equivalent.reason: Removing the subtraction of refunded_amount allows refunding more than the actual remaining captured amount, letting cumulative refunds exceed captured_amount and violating INV-3.

mutants.desc: Swaps source/destination on the capture ledger pair, debiting the merchant and crediting the holds account instead of the reverse, inverting fund flow on every capture.
mutants.expect: Ledger pair direction for CAPTURE must debit ACCT_HOLDS and credit the merchant account

equivalent.reason: Swapping debit/credit reverses the CAPTURE ledger direction, causing merchant balance to decrease and ACCT_HOLDS to increase instead of vice versa, which is observable via get_balance.

mutants.desc: Swaps source/destination on the authorization hold, crediting external settlement and debiting holds instead of the reverse, inverting the hold's ledger effect.
mutants.expect: Ledger pair direction for AUTHORIZE_HOLD must debit ACCT_EXTERNAL_SETTLEMENT and credit ACCT_HOLDS

equivalent.reason: Swapping debit/credit reverses the ledger direction for AUTHORIZE_HOLD, causing balance_of queries on ACCT_HOLDS and ACCT_EXTERNAL_SETTLEMENT to return opposite-sign observable results.

mutants.desc: Positive-amount validation now accepts zero, allowing zero-amount intents, captures, and refunds that should be rejected.
mutants.expect: Amounts subject to _require_positive must be an integer >= 1 (zero is invalid)

equivalent.reason: With amount=0, original raises ValidationError while mutated allows 0 through, changing behavior for e.g. create_intent(amount=0).

mutants.desc: Allows a capture amount exactly equal to the fee, letting a capture net zero to the merchant after the platform fee, violating the 'must be strictly greater than fee' business rule.
mutants.expect: Capture amount must be strictly greater than the capture fee (amount > fee), not merely >= fee

equivalent.reason: When amount == fee, the original raises ValidationError but the mutated code does not, changing observable behavior for that input.

mutants.desc: Permits voiding an intent that has already been fully CAPTURED, a terminal-adjacent state that should only be reachable via refund, letting a captured payment be voided and its hold 'released' after funds already moved to the merchant.
mutants.expect: State machine: void is only valid from CREATED, AUTHORIZED, or PARTIALLY_CAPTURED, never from CAPTURED

equivalent.reason: Now voiding a CAPTURED intent succeeds instead of raising InvalidStateError, directly violating the stated invariant.

mutants.desc: Fingerprint drops the endpoint from the hashed payload, so the same idempotency key with an identical payload but a different endpoint (e.g. capture vs refund with the same {id, amount}) is treated as a valid replay instead of a conflict.
mutants.expect: Idempotency fingerprint must bind the key to both endpoint and payload; cross-endpoint reuse of a key must raise IdempotencyConflictError

equivalent.reason: Removing endpoint from the fingerprint means the same key with the same payload but different endpoint yields the same fingerprint, so cross-endpoint reuse will be treated as a valid replay instead of raising IdempotencyConflictError.

mutants.desc: Draws the capture fee from the merchant account instead of external settlement, contradicting ADR-0005 and causing a full refund to leave the merchant balance negative by the fee amount instead of returning it to zero.
mutants.expect: ADR-0005: capture fee must be debited from ACCT_EXTERNAL_SETTLEMENT, never from the merchant account (INV-3: merchant balance never negative, full refund returns merchant to zero)
